senses.definition: used to describe a sudden departure or a hasty retreat

examples.x: I had to make a twenty-three skidoo when my boss walked in on me taking a nap at my desk.
The party was getting out of hand, so we decided to make a twenty-three skidoo and leave before things got any worse.

senses.definition: to leave a place or move really fast or with haste

examples.x: We need to haul ass if we want to catch the last train.
I had to haul ass to the meeting because I was running late.
The fire alarm went off, and everyone started hauling ass out of the building.
If you want to make it to the sale on time, you better haul ass to the store.
When the bell rang, the students quickly hauled ass out of the classroom.

senses.definition: to do something very hurriedly, often used when demanding someone to move or leave quickly

examples.x: Come on, move your ass, we're running late!
I told him to move his ass, but he took his sweet time.
If you want to catch the train, you'll need to move your ass.
We were in a rush, so we were constantly telling each other to move our asses.
"By the time he finally moved his ass, it was too late.

senses.definition: someone who is late for getting somewhere and anxiously tries to get there

examples.x: They were acting like white rabbits, frantically searching for their misplaced keys.

senses.definition: as quickly as one possibly can because there is not much time available

examples.x: We are working against the clock to finish the project before the deadline.
They raced against the clock and completed the task just in time.
She will be working against the clock to meet the upcoming deadline.
They were working against the clock all night to fix the technical issues.
By this time tomorrow, we will be racing against the clock to finalize the preparations.

senses.definition: to complete a task or achieve a goal before a deadline or a specific time limit

examples.x: We are trying to beat the clock and finish the task before the deadline.
They beat the clock and completed the project just in time.
She will be trying to beat the clock to meet the upcoming deadline.
By this time tomorrow, we will be trying to beat the clock to finish the preparations.
I have been trying to beat the clock to get everything done.

senses.definition: to manage to do something just before the time is over

examples.x: He is cutting it close with his presentation; he only has five minutes left to finish.
They cut it close with their flight; they arrived at the airport just minutes before the departure.
I will be cutting things close with the deadline; I expect to submit the report right before it's due.
She has cut it close multiple times, but somehow always manages to meet the deadline.

senses.definition: to do something more quickly in order not to be late

examples.x: Get a move on or we'll be late for the movie!
We got a move on and managed to catch the last train.
They were getting a move on when the storm hit, forcing them to seek shelter.
I have finally gotten a move on with my fitness routine.

senses.definition: to begin doing something much more quickly

examples.x: You need to get your ass into gear if you want to meet the deadline.
He finally got his ass in gear and finished the project before the cut-off date.
Tomorrow, she will have to get her ass in gear to prepare for the big presentation.
They were getting their asses in gear when the supervisor walked in.

senses.definition: to do something too earlier than its right time

examples.x: Don't jump the gun and make a decision before considering all the options.
She jumped the gun and started celebrating before realizing she hadn't actually won the competition.
Tomorrow, they will likely jump the gun and announce the results before the official announcement.
Why are you jumping the gun?
They were jumping the gun by assuming the project was a success before it was completed.

senses.definition: said as a way of asking someone to begin doing something or continue doing it faster than before

examples.x: Come on, shake a leg, we don't want to be late for the movie.
Tomorrow morning, we'll need to shake a leg to catch our flight on time.
Whenever we were running late, she would always tell us to shake a leg and move faster.

senses.definition: used as a warning to remind one that the time that is left for doing something is about to be over
to begin hurrying up or moving faster

examples.x: Get the lead out and finish that report before the deadline!
We got the lead out and completed the project just in time.
Tomorrow, we will need to get the lead out and prepare for the upcoming presentation.
He's getting the lead out and working diligently to meet the production targets.
We were getting the lead out all night, trying to fix the technical issues before the event.

word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɹˈeɪs ɐɡˈɛnst tˈaɪm ðə klˈɑːk

senses.definition: used for describing a situation when there is only a little time available and one must hurry up in order to get something done

examples.x: They are in a race against time to finish the project before the deadline.
We were in a race against time to catch the last train.
Tomorrow, they will be in a race against time to complete the rescue mission.
They are currently in a race against time to find a cure for the disease.
During that period, they were in a race against time to save the trapped miners.
By this time next month, they will be in a race against time to launch the new product.

senses.definition: used to say that something is done with great speed and determination

examples.x: He's got the pedal to the metal as he works tirelessly to complete the project ahead of schedule.
They had the pedal to the metal and achieved their sales targets for the previous quarter.
She's currently got the pedal to the metal, training intensely for the upcoming marathon.
They were driving with the pedal to the metal, trying to catch the last flight out of the airport.
By this time next year, they will still be putting the pedal to the metal to expand their business operations.
